codeReview.summary: All three sub-features are implemented and function correctly for the interactive use case. The GitHub header button is placed correctly with proper SVG, URL, and CSS. The clear X button is wired up correctly and shows/hides on inputValue. The token label restructure works: QuoteForm tracks fromTokenInfo/toTokenInfo and renders icon+symbol in label rows; Autocomplete returns address-only from formatTokenDisplay. One non-blocking initialization timing bug exists where fromTokenInfo/toTokenInfo are null on initial page load (before tokenlist loads), so label icon+symbol is hidden at startup for default pairs. One dead-code nit in formatTokenDisplay. One broad CSS selector change warrants a note.

issues.description: fromTokenInfo and toTokenInfo are set synchronously in the chain $effect (which runs during initial render, before App.svelte's onMount awaits initTokenlists()). At that point _tokenIndex is an empty Map, so getTokenFromIndex() returns null. There is no reactive $effect in QuoteForm that re-evaluates these when $tokenIndex changes. Result: on initial page load, the label row shows 'From Token' / 'To Token' without any icon or symbol for the default pair, even though Autocomplete's own $effect (which does track $tokenIndex) will correctly update the input. The label stays stale/null until the user explicitly interacts with the field. The worker's browser verification only checked after a clear+reselect, not the initial state. A fix would be to add a $effect in QuoteForm that re-resolves fromTokenInfo/toTokenInfo when $tokenIndex changes (similar to how Autocomplete retries with the void $tokenIndex pattern).

formatTokenDisplay is dead code: both the if(symbol) branch and the fallback return the same value (`address`). The function could be simplified to `return address;`. The spec said 'keep SYMBOL (address) format only for unresolved tokens' — however, formatTokenDisplay is only ever called from selectToken() where tokens always have a symbol (they come from the tokenlist), so the 'no symbol' branch is unreachable in practice. Functionally correct, but confusing.

The label rule was changed from `display: block` to `display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; flex-wrap: wrap;` globally for ALL label elements in the app. This is a broad selector change (no scoping class). Currently, non-token labels (Amount, Block) have only a single text child so the visual impact is minimal. Future labels with multi-word content or nested elements could be affected unintentionally. The intent was to support inline icon+symbol in token labels; scoping this to a `.token-field-label` class would be safer.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill's Step 4 ('Write Tests First - TDD') requires tests to be written before implementation. For this UI-only feature the worker skipped TDD entirely, citing lack of a component-test harness for Svelte markup/CSS interactions. The worker flagged this in skillFeedback.deviations. The deviation was justified — the repo has no component-level UI tests and only pure-logic unit tests. The skill should be updated to clarify that TDD applies to logic functions; markup/CSS interactions may be verified via svelte-check + build + browser verification instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false, deviations[0].step='4. Write Tests First (TDD)'; frontend/src/lib/components/ has no *.test.ts files — only frontend/src/lib/stores/*.test.ts and api.test.ts cover logic, not component rendering.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Global `label` CSS selector was broadened (display:flex) to support the new token label layout. The project pattern is to use scoped CSS classes for component-specific styling. Changing the global label rule to flex could unintentionally affect future label elements. AGENTS.md and mission AGENTS.md don't document a convention about global vs scoped label styles. A note about preferring scoped classes for label variants would prevent similar drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/app.css line 354 — `label { display: flex; ... }` (global selector); all other layout overrides in that file use class selectors (.autocomplete-wrapper, .token-input-wrapper, etc.)

summary: All three UI improvements are correctly implemented and all formal validators pass (build, svelte-check, 78 tests). GitHub button is correctly positioned with proper SVG icon, URL, and 36x36 sizing. Clear X button shows/hides on inputValue state and properly notifies parent. Token label restructure is implemented with icon+symbol in label row and address-only in input. One non-blocking initialization timing bug: fromTokenInfo/toTokenInfo are not reactively re-evaluated when $tokenIndex loads, so label icon+symbol is invisible on initial page load (only becomes visible after user interacts). Dead code in formatTokenDisplay is a minor nit. Overall the feature satisfies its expectedBehavior for interactive use cases and all verification steps pass.
